reasoning: Both outputs are strong and pass all 10 expectations. B edges out A on correctness and completeness: it found 4 bugs (vs A's 3), correctly identifying the unbounded SELECT as a blocking bug rather than a non-blocking improvement — a genuine architectural risk at scale. B's failure tracker is more accurate, recording actual failure counts per check (M7: 1/3, PC-S2 probe: 1/3, etc.) rather than all-zeros as in A, which creates a misleading picture given that bugs were found. B also re-runs mechanical checks post-fix (showing iteration 1 and iteration 2 states), providing stronger proof of green state. B's recycle table explicitly tracks PC count progression (14→15→16→17→18), demonstrating monotonic progress more concretely. A is stronger on evidence richness — it includes full RED/GREEN test code snippets and more precise line-number references in bug descriptions — which is valuable but secondary to finding and correctly classifying bugs. The tie-breaker is bug count and tracker accuracy: B found a real additional bug (unbounded SELECT escalated to BUG-4) and its failure tracker reflects actual test outcomes rather than all-pass counts that contradict the bugs found.

strengths: Full RED/GREEN test code snippets provided for all 3 bugs — concrete proof of TDD recycle loop
ASCII summary boxes make pass/fail status instantly scannable
Contract probing table includes 'Original Test' column, making the delta from adversarial probing explicit
All 5 adversarial lens findings labelled and clearly separated as blocking vs non-blocking
Bug descriptions include approximate line references (e.g., 'line ~18')
weaknesses: Failure tracker shows all-zero failure counts (0/3 for every check) despite 3 bugs being found — this is internally inconsistent and misleading
Mechanical checks only reported for a single iteration (post-fix state not confirmed separately)
Treated unbounded SELECT as a non-blocking improvement rather than a blocking scalability bug — under-classified one real bug
3 bugs found vs 4 in B — missed the module-level mutable state bug (retryCount) at the mechanical check stage

strengths: Found 4 bugs vs A's 3 — correctly escalated unbounded SELECT (BUG-4) to blocking and module-level mutable state (BUG-1/retryCount) from M7
Failure tracker accurately records actual failures per check (M7: 1/3, PC-S2: 1/3, etc.) — reflects reality
Mechanical checks re-run for both iterations (pre-fix and post-fix states both documented)
Recycle table shows PC count at each iteration (14→15→16→17→18) proving monotonic contract expansion
Non-blocking improvements in a dedicated numbered section (Section 6) with IDs for tracking
weaknesses: Bug descriptions reference files but fewer explicit line numbers compared to A
No inline RED/GREEN code snippets — recycle loop evidence is tabular rather than code-level, requiring more trust
Adversarial lens section is more compact and less granular per-lens than A
